items.title: NT councils could cut access to pools, street lights due to power price hike

children.content: Power price hikes are expected to be passed onto local councils by the Northern Territory government in the coming month, with Darwin's lord mayor warning some community services could be cut as a result.

image.altText: A sign outside an old building reads: City of Darwin.

items.title: Not all city suburbs have NBN access and it isn't by accident. Here's why

children.content: Despite popular belief, not all Australians are guaranteed access to the NBN — and as many consumers have discovered, alternative networks do not always provide quality service.

image.altText: A graphic showing a person's hands on a laptop with a Wifi failure symbol superimposed over the top.

avatar.alt: An Australian-Egyptian woman with dark brown shoulder-length curly hair smiling at camera, in a green patterned top.

items.title: Flood-damaged roads cause 'catastrophic' impacts for NT businesses

children.content: Workers in road-reliant industries are calling on the federal government to provide urgent funding to repair the NT's road network, which was severely damaged by floods.

gallery.altText: A drone shot shows a damaged dirt road alongside a brown river, with a massive pothole missing from one section.
Construction workers on a road with recently filled potholes
A man and woman looking seriously at the camera while standing in front of trucks parked at a depot.

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot shows a damaged dirt road alongside a brown river, with a massive pothole missing from one section.
Construction workers on a road with recently filled potholes
A man and woman looking seriously at the camera while standing in front of trucks parked at a depot.

items.title: 'Derek' the dingo a concern for some as he roams streets in outback town

children.content: An lone dingo, affectionately known as Derek, has been spotted in suburban streets, backyards and even in dog parks, raising some concern for Alice Springs residents.

image.altText: A dingo walking on a street

items.title: Former middle school teacher jailed for striking student in the face

children.content: The court heard Darroch Robinson struck the girl after she insulted him following the confiscation of her phone, causing her to fall into a table and break her teeth.

image.altText: A man in a shirt and tie leaves court

items.title: CLP, Labor have 'blood on their hands' after remote death, MLA says

children.content: An independent member of the Northern Territory parliament says successive NT governments "have blood on their hands", following the death of a 26-year-old man in a remote Arnhem Land community this week.

image.altText: A photo of an Indigenous man in a suit, speaking outside NT Parliament.

items.title: Australian bush tea hits shelves in Asia

children.content: The product is being sent from a remote property in the Northern Territory all the way to Japan, Korea and China.

image.altText: A close up image of tea leaves in a pot.

items.title: Uncle 'worried and scared' after fatally stabbing sistergirl, court hears

children.content: The partner of a man charged with the stabbing murder of a Tiwi Islands sistergirl tells the NT Supreme Court she saw him run outside "like a flash" before crying and repeatedly calling out "call the police".

gallery.altText: A First Nations man wearing mirrored sunglasses, pointing at himself.

thumbnail.altText: A First Nations man wearing mirrored sunglasses, pointing at himself.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a pattern blouse and serious facial expression.

items.title: Major upgrades planned for 200-hectare site once used as TV, film backdrop

children.content: Australian-based global tour operator Intrepid has announced a multi-million-dollar investment in Central Australia's Ooraminna Homestead, 30 kilometres south of Alice Springs.

image.altText: A wide angle shot of a small tin cabin nestled among trees in a rocky outback landscape.

items.title: Woman suffers brain injury after 'toxic dose' of wrong drug

children.content: A 36-year-old woman who says she suffered a "hypoxic brain injury" when she was given a "toxic dose" of the wrong drug during routine surgery at Palmerston Regional Hospital is suing the Northern Territory government for negligence.

image.altText: A patient laying on a hospital bed, knees up with blanket covering their legs, holding a cup next to urine sample container

items.title: Why Australian tea tree oil could be banned in Europe

children.content: The EU is considering banning Australian tea tree oil after a study raised concerns about potential reproductive risks, a move industry leaders warn could devastate exports and trigger global impacts.

gallery.altText: A bottle of tea tree solution from brand Eureka.
small bottles of tea tree oils from company Thursday Plantation.
wide shot of tea tree plantation farm land

thumbnail.altText: A bottle of tea tree solution from brand Eureka.
small bottles of tea tree oils from company Thursday Plantation.
wide shot of tea tree plantation farm land

items.title: Suns hold off Bombers in thriller, Bulldogs star out for season

children.content: The Gold Coast Suns dig deep to hold off a confident Essendon side to win by nine points, while the Western Bulldogs' worst fears are realised. Follow live.

image.altText: Christian Petracca celebrates a goal for the Suns.

items.title: Warriors finishing tries in style to take big lead over Titans

children.content: The New Zealand Warriors scored four tries in a dominant first half over the Gold Coast Titans in Auckland. Follow live.

image.altText: Winger dives and grounds the ball with one hand

items.title: Iran's water crisis is worse than you think

children.content: As air strikes hit the Persian Gulf,
attacks on Iran’s water infrastructure
risk worsening drought, protests, and civilian suffering.
Desalination plants which turn seawater into drinking water have become military targets.
An entire river has gone dry in central Iran.
Iran’s water crisis may shape the country’s future more than the conflict itself.

image.altText: Matt Bevan is shown behind a microphone in a studio with purplish lighting

gallery.altText: Matt Bevan is shown behind a microphone in a studio with purplish lighting

thumbnail.altText: Matt Bevan is shown behind a microphone in a studio with purplish lighting

items.title: The world faces a grim reality, even if Trump exists in a different one

children.content: The world is stuck watching a performative series of announcements about meetings that could, may, will happen, involving a president not dealing with the reality that he is in a weak and confused bargaining position.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands outside the White House as reporters hold microphones.

items.title: Australia enters drone warfare era with Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark

children.content: Australia is on a push to keep pace with the rapid evolution of drone warfare, with an emphasis on locally produced Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark systems.

image.altText: A black mini submarine looking drone seen underwater.

items.title: The Windsor playbook with a twist: Harry and Meghan's royal model

children.content: Mixing philanthropy, influencing and brand building, Harry and Meghan’s Australian tour revives a question Queen Elizabeth once shut down: can the 'half in, half out' royal life work?

image.altText: Harry and Meghan Australian tour

items.title: Gout celebrates early to win another title, says magic mark 'coming soon'

children.content: Gout Gout waves goodbye to his opponents en route to another national under-20 title in the 100m, as the wait goes on for him to dip below the 10-second barrier.

image.altText: Gout Gout waves at an opponent in the national under-20 100m final.

items.title: Skip the takeaway. This halloumi wrap is quicker and healthy too

children.content: The only cooking in this simple dinner is the halloumi. It has takeaway vibes and just the right balance of flavours to become a family favourite.

gallery.altText: A round flatbread on a kitchen bench with salad and grilled halloumi filling in the centre.

thumbnail.altText: A round flatbread on a kitchen bench with salad and grilled halloumi filling in the centre.

items.title: Old is new again: Gen alpha takes to old tech with fresh eyes

children.content: With the constant evolution of the digital world, community history is rapidly disappearing into memories and museums. But a historical society on WA's south coast is delighting today's kids by showing them the technology older Australians grew up with.

image.altText: A young girl looks into the rear of a bellows camera

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of the Big Day Out, footy fairytales and the circles of hell

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

image.altText: The three members of Nirvana pose for a photo.

items.title: Desperate for social media ban to work, but parents say it’s 'impossible'

children.content: Tech companies are coming under fire as Australian parents increasingly voice concerns that some online platforms are failing to keep kids under 16 off their platforms.

gallery.altText: close up of social media apps on iphone screen including facebook, messenger, tiktok, instagram, threads and whatsapp

thumbnail.altText: close up of social media apps on iphone screen including facebook, messenger, tiktok, instagram, threads and whatsapp

items.title: Outcry over 'disturbing' video of off-duty police officer pinning 13yo girl on train

children.content: Video of an off-duty police officer restraining a 13yo girl on a Perth train has sparked accusations of excessive force.

image.altText: A young girl holds her head and winces with her face being blurred.

items.title: Patients with little-known neurological condition wait years for diagnosis

children.content: People with functional neurological disorder say they experience misdiagnosis and struggles within the medical system.

image.altText: A woman stands smiling and holding her arms out in front of large red rocks.

items.title: Two words threaten to derail Minns's plan to ban controversial phrase

children.content: NSW Premier Chris Minns must now decide whether to proceed with the next restrictions he hoped to impose on protests after his latest court defeat.

image.altText: NSW Premier Chris Minns

avatar.alt: Profile picture ABC reporter Alex Lewis

items.title: Toddler's death at Perth hospital was 'probably preventable'

children.content: The death of a toddler at one of Perth's major hospitals was "probably preventable" and the failure to perform a blood test on him was "more than a missed opportunity", a coroner finds.

image.altText: A close up shot of a child smiling

items.title: Their two families are fabulously wealthy, but it hasn't brought harmony

children.content: Their fathers were school buddies turned mining pioneers, and in another life they could have been friends — but Gina Rinehart and Angela Bennett came to be opponents in one of the most high-stakes legal battles Australia has ever seen.

image.altText: A background image of a mine site, with pictures of four mining figures from left to right.

items.title: Why global stocks keep heading into orbit

children.content: The Australian Securities Exchange is within striking distance of new records as economists issue worst-case warnings of a close call for a global recession.

image.altText: Pedestrians are reflected in a window with an indicator board displaying stock prices at the Australian Stock Exchange in Sydney

items.title: An executive at FIFA's new betting partner settled an insider-trading lawsuit

children.content: The backstory of the prediction market industry's newest entrant has done little to calm concerns as it partners with several giants of sports broadcasting.

gallery.altText: Collage graphic featuring picture of man wearing business suit, football players, share chart and cryptocurrency.

thumbnail.altText: Collage graphic featuring picture of man wearing business suit, football players, share chart and cryptocurrency.

items.title: The PM is not about to pick a fight over a gas tax

children.content: The budget's usual timing on the second Tuesday of May is an artifice of political convention. This year's edition just happens to be coinciding with the thunderclap of history in flux.

image.altText: A close up of Albanese, who wears a neutral expression

items.title: Long before Dezi Freeman, other criminals hid along the Upper Murray

children.content: Dezi Freeman used Victoria's Upper Murray region to hide, but it has long been a popular location for those trying to avoid capture.

image.altText: a man in a flat cap holds a black and white newspaper clipping

items.title: National 'Walk for Truth' asks PM to deliver on long-awaited promise

children.content: Indigenous leader Travis Lovett sets off on a trek to Canberra, calling for action from the federal government to support a national truth-telling process for First Nations people.

gallery.altText: Commissioner Travis Lovett raising a message stick on the steps of Parliament House with a crowd of supporters behind him.

thumbnail.altText: Commissioner Travis Lovett raising a message stick on the steps of Parliament House with a crowd of supporters behind him.

avatar.alt: A reporter stands in front of a city street as she prepares to speak to the camera

items.title: Jess is a champion boxer but she has to pay for her fights

children.content: Jess Messina is an Australian champion but she has dropped a weight class to find a fight and struggles to find promoters prepared to back her financially.

image.altText: Female boxer in blue punches toward camera, a determined look and sweat on her brow.

items.title: Popular outback motoring rally cancelled due to fuel crisis

children.content: As fuel becomes less available, Variety NSW is concerned its main fundraising event could harm the small communities it tries to support.

image.altText: A car decorated like Thomas the Tank Engine driving through the outback

items.title: How Maltese migrants united to build a Queensland sugarcane legacy

children.content: Long before they were cast in bronze, a group of Maltese migrants quietly reshaped who would own farmland across north Queensland.

gallery.altText: A closeup of the face of a bronze statue of a man

thumbnail.altText: A closeup of the face of a bronze statue of a man
